enrichmentScore.reasoning: The assistant created aspects for work/health/social but did not capture distinct school details (CS 161, CS 221, MATH 120) and also created duplicate/incorrect aspects (both “Blue Bottle” and “Work”). Recurring events are largely wrong/misaligned (times/dates don’t match the user’s stated MWF/TTh patterns, and key events like CS classes, Sunday study group, and Saturday pickup basketball are missing), and the conversation/tool usage appears to have tool-call issues and over/under-capture. Goals were partially captured, but the resulting calendar would not be functional for the user’s actual schedule.

scheduleScore.reasoning: All four suggestions clearly reference the user’s stated goals (3.8 GPA, half marathon in October, saving $2000) and the CS 161 study group, with specific “why” descriptions. However, no placement slots were produced (0 slots), so nothing was actually added to the calendar, leaving the schedule largely unchanged and unbalanced with no conflict/gap validation possible.

conversationScore.reasoning: Tool usage is inconsistent with the criteria: free-time queries didn’t call find_free_time, task creation used the wrong tool (create_task missing), and the duplicate-check scenario failed to use list_events. The assistant also shows anti-pattern risks like creating overlapping/duplicate events without reliably checking existing schedule, and some responses reference incorrect time handling (e.g., dentist appointment at 2pm becoming 2am).

character.name: Marcus Chen

goals: Run a half marathon in October

character.personality: You are Marcus Chen, a 22-year-old CS junior at Stanford. You are friendly but concise -- you answer questions directly without over-explaining. You sometimes use casual language like "yeah" and "nah". You volunteer schedule details when asked but don't dump everything at once. You're a bit stressed about balancing school and work but generally upbeat.
enrichmentDetails: I take CS 161 (Algorithms) MWF 10:30am-11:20am in Gates B01 with Professor Roughgarden
I take CS 221 (AI) TTh 1:30pm-2:50pm in Hewlett 200 with Professor Liang
I take MATH 120 (Groups and Rings) MWF 1:30pm-2:20pm in room 380-380C
I work at Blue Bottle Coffee on campus, Tuesday and Thursday mornings 6am-10am, and Saturday 7am-1pm
I go to the gym (lifting) Monday, Wednesday, Friday around 7:30am before class. Usually takes about an hour
I run on Tuesday and Thursday evenings around 6pm, training for a half marathon
I have a study group for CS 161 on Sunday afternoons from 2-5pm at Green Library
I try to do problem sets on weekday evenings, usually 7-10pm
I wake up around 6:30am on weekdays and go to bed around midnight
I play pickup basketball with friends on Saturday afternoons

suggestions.description: Your goal is to run a half marathon in October. This session creates a realistic weekly training plan (runs + cross-training + rest) based on your current running schedule, so you know exactly what to do each week from now through October.

suggestions.title: Set a GPA target plan: map this semester’s remaining work to 3.8
suggestions.description: Your goal is to get a 3.8 GPA this semester. This session turns that target into a concrete plan by listing remaining assignments/exams, estimating their impact, and identifying which courses/units need the most focus so you can prioritize effectively.

suggestions.title: Build a “save $2000 by summer” budget sprint (next 6 weeks)
suggestions.description: Your goal is to save $2000 by summer. This session creates a concrete budget sprint: set a weekly savings target, identify the top 3 adjustable expenses, and draft a simple plan to hit the number without last-minute scrambling.

suggestions.title: CS 161: plan your next study-group prep (topics + questions)
suggestions.description: You have a CS 161 Study Group on Sun Apr 12. This session prepares you by identifying what you need to review, drafting 3–5 questions, and outlining the key topics you’ll bring so you get more value from the group (supporting your 3.8 GPA goal).

behaviorCriteria: [Enrichment] Agent must create aspects for each distinct life area mentioned (school, work, hobbies, health)
[Enrichment] Agent must create recurring events with correct days/times when user shares schedule details
[Enrichment] Agent must NOT create duplicate aspects or events for the same activity
[Enrichment] Agent must ask about ALL life areas (school/work, health, personal, daily routine) across the conversation
[Enrichment] Agent must call tools SILENTLY -- no narrating "I will now create an aspect for..."
[Enrichment] Agent must keep responses to 2-4 sentences, no markdown, no bullet points
[Enrichment] Agent must create aspect FIRST, then event using that aspect (correct sequencing)
[Enrichment] Agent must capture goals mentioned by user via create_goal tool
[Enrichment] Agent must update profile with occupation, work hours, preferences when shared
[Enrichment] Agent should cover all 5 areas within the turn budget (school, work, health, personal, routine)
[Conversation] Schedule queries ("what do I have today") must call list_events or search_events, not create anything
[Conversation] Event creation must use the correct aspect for the activity type
[Conversation] Event modification must search for the existing event first, then update it -- not create a new one
[Conversation] Event deletion must confirm the right event before deleting (search first)
[Conversation] Agent must NOT create duplicate events when one already exists at that time
[Conversation] Agent must be aware of existing schedule when suggesting times ("do I have time for X")
[Conversation] Task creation should use create_task, not create_event for non-time-bound items
[Conversation] Goal check-ins should call list_goals or check_in_goal, not create new goals
[Conversation] Responses must be concise and natural -- no markdown headers, no numbered lists unless asked
[Conversation] Agent must not call unnecessary tools -- a simple greeting needs zero tool calls
[Conversation] Agent must handle ambiguous time references correctly ("tomorrow", "next Monday", "this afternoon")
[Conversation] When asked about free time, agent must call find_free_time and give specific windows
[Scheduler] Every suggestion must reference a specific goal, task, or aspect -- no generic filler
[Scheduler] Suggestion descriptions must explain WHY (reference the goal/task it advances)
[Scheduler] High-energy tasks (deep work, studying) must be placed in mornings/early afternoon
[Scheduler] Low-energy tasks (review, planning) must be placed in evenings
[Scheduler] No overlapping slots with existing events
[Scheduler] Minimum 60-minute duration for all slots
[Scheduler] Maximum 3-4 slots per day, spread across 7 days
[Scheduler] Multiple life areas must be represented (not all one aspect)
[Scheduler] Slots must have 30+ minute gaps between them and existing events
[Anti-pattern] DUPLICATE CREATION: Creating a second "CS 161 Lecture" when one already exists at MWF 10:30am
[Anti-pattern] TOOL NARRATION: "I will now create an aspect for your class" instead of just doing it
[Anti-pattern] GENERIC SUGGESTIONS: "Watch a TED talk" or "Go for a walk" with no goal connection
[Anti-pattern] OVER-TOOLING: Calling 5 tools when 1 would suffice
[Anti-pattern] UNDER-TOOLING: Responding to "add a meeting" with text instead of calling create_event
[Anti-pattern] WRONG TOOL: Using create_event for a task, or create_task for a scheduled meeting
[Anti-pattern] MARKDOWN IN CHAT: Using ## headers, **bold**, - bullet points in conversational responses
[Anti-pattern] IGNORING CONTEXT: Suggesting a meeting at 2pm when user has class then
[Anti-pattern] ASPECT ORPHANS: Creating events without linking to an aspect
[Anti-pattern] STALE RESPONSES: Not reflecting the current state of the calendar in answers
